justifications.ip1: AC1 covered by T1 with specific field assertions ('group', 'dimension', 'gap', 'threshold', 'pass'), AC2 by T2 (exit 1 with threshold=0.05 vs gap=1.0) and T3 (stderr contains threshold value and dimension names), AC3 by T4 including boundary test (gap==threshold exits 0).
justifications.ip2: No out-of-scope items implemented; 'What will NOT be built' list (retraining, CCCFA, Gini/ROC-AUC/KS, real-time inference, database) is entirely absent from the plan, and all three mandated files (evaluate_fairness.py, test_evaluate_fairness.py, fairness-config.json) are present.
justifications.ip3: Every task names specific files and provides complete code with concrete assertions — e.g. T5 asserts 'abs(entry["gap"] - 0.50) < 1e-9' for gender gap, T7 uses exact threshold values 0.05 and 0.10 to detect hardcoding.
justifications.ip4: TASK 1 writes all failing tests first with explicit RED verification ('Run pytest... all tests must fail with FileNotFoundError or ModuleNotFoundError'); TASK 3 explicitly states 'Tests must already exist and be failing before this task is started.'
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 has T5 verifying exact max-min formula (gender gap=0.50, ethnicity gap=0.00); NFR-2 has T6 (identical stdout across runs); C1 is enforced by T7 (two-run test with different config thresholds) and by CONFIG_PATH constant with no hardcoded numeric threshold anywhere in the implementation; C2 has T3 verifying stderr on every run.

notes: Exemplary plan: all ACs have precise numerical test scenarios, T7 provides a definitive C1 (no-hardcoding) test by varying the config threshold between runs, and the implementation correctly reads the threshold from fairness-config.json at runtime with no hardcoded numeric values.
